a:r.a:t: Reconciliation Bridge | Top-down ($17.0B) and bottom-up ($9.0B) reconcile across 7 bridge components, with Public Naval Shipyard labor ($7.49B) the structural FPDS-invisible gap
Primary recon test (plan section 6.5). FY2025 PB26 forecast vs FPDS actual obligations
STRUCTURAL: federal civilian workforce, FPDS-invisible by construction
OPN LI 1000 Ship Maint Repair and Mod
NEW source-pinned. Modernization side of avails, separate appropriation from OMN
MSC M and R (1B1B FY2025 to 1B4B FY2026)
MSC HQ contracting flow. Reconciles within plus or minus 15%
Multi-year incremental funding vs single-FY FPDS obligation. PSC 1905 sees more
TD anchor (ISVS PC and I floor) is known low. FPDS sees actual cutter awards
Other (RDT and E-DW Strategic, AF/Army cross-svc, etc.)
BU captures Strategic Systems (Draper Trident), AF/Army flows. Not in OP-5/MSC/SCN/USCG anchors
Public NSY $7,485M + Private TD $9,511M vs BU $8,971M = TD private +$540M (~6%)

Source: workbook_build TAM Bridge sheet (corrected 2026-04-27); plan section 2.3 dual-narrative reconciliation. Top-down components fully source-pinned to budget-book line items after OPN LI 1000 substitution. WPN combat-systems sustainment intentionally excluded from top-down (no Table-IV-equivalent exists in WPN P-40 exhibits); FPDS captures the WPN flow on combat-system PSCs (J012, J014, K010, K012) within Narrative A. Data as of April 2026.
